summary: Iteration 2 backend + frontend testing. Pytest: 25/25 passing (auth, roles, users, products gating, transactions, brute-force lockout). Playwright: login (no Register toggle), admin dashboard with 3 tabs, product CRUD via UI, transaction dialog, account creation, transactions tab with filters/stats, mobile action bar (<768px) opening transaction dialog, personnel role correctly hides accounts tab and admin-only inventory controls, theme toggle, and logout all working.

minor.issue: Email is silently lowercased server-side. Not a bug per se, but should be documented; tests using mixed-case emails initially mismatched on response.

issues: No mobile-card list testid coverage was needed but checked. Visual: 'Activity log' header well aligned. Looks clean.

action_items: Optional cleanup: prune residual TEST_* products and users that may remain in DB from earlier iterations (not blocking; pytest fixtures clean up their own).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: emails normalised to lowercase on create — good. Continue normalising on login lookup (already done).
Brute-force lockout uses X-Forwarded-For + email key; verified by test producing 429 on 6th wrong attempt.
Transactions endpoint atomically updates stock & rejects oversell (verified). Consider returning Pydantic model with explicit response_model to keep field set stable.
Frontend: Dashboard mounts InventoryTab which fetches products via parent; mobile-action-bar uses md:hidden Tailwind breakpoint (768px) — matches spec.
data-testid coverage is excellent across pages (login, dashboard, tabs, dialogs, table).
test_credentials: Admin: admin@example.com / admin123. Created personnel via UI: test_uiclerk@example.com / clerk123 (lowercased by server).
seed_data_creation: Created TEST_UI_Prod_X (qty 12, $9.50, TEST_CAT) via UI for visual validation. Pytest fixtures create and clean up their own TEST_* products and users.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend stable on iteration 2. Roles + transactions fully wired. Note: emails are server-normalised to lowercase — assert against .lower() of the input email. Brute-force tests must use Session() without prior auth so the X-Forwarded-For + email key triggers lockout. Mobile viewport = 390x844; mobile-action-bar uses md:hidden so anything <768px shows it.
rca_of_issues: No production bugs found. Two initial pytest failures were test-only assertions against original-case emails; fixed by using lowercase in test data to match server normalisation.
